http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#defi.@value: The Hazard Type vocabulary is a SKOS concept scheme for describing the nature of hazards associated with specific materials, with coverage spanning the biological and geological collections domains.

http://purl.org/dc/terms/description.@value: The Hazard Type vocabulary is a SKOS concept scheme for describing the nature of hazards associated with specific materials, with coverage spanning the biological and geological collections domains.

http://purl.org/dc/terms/provenance.@value: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group to support community use and the new term hazardType, released as part of a broader set of terms extending Darwin Core coverage to mineralogical and geological collections.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#scop.@value: Despite being published by the Mineralogy Extension Task Group, the hazard type concept scheme is intended to support specimens across that belong to biological and geological collections.

http://purl.org/vocab/vann/usageNote.@value: Intended to be used in conjuncture with the proposed darwin core term, hazardType

https://schema.org/citation.@value: Norton B, Walcott R, Burri T, Puschnig A, Dombrowski A, Greber N, Hints O, Mansur A, Ralph J, Richard S, Tacker C. Hazard Type Vocabulary (pending).

https://prez.dev/description.@value: The Hazard Type vocabulary is a SKOS concept scheme for describing the nature of hazards associated with specific materials, with coverage spanning the biological and geological collections domains.

https://prez.dev/provenance.@value: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group to support community use and the new term hazardType, released as part of a broader set of terms extending Darwin Core coverage to mineralogical and geological collections.
